Explanation: Actively joining raids advances her police career and helps bring criminals to justice.
Surveilling Vondas yields key case information and disrupts organized crime.

Event: Uses her union relationships to get key information
Explanation: Leveraging trust with union members aids the case and benefits public safety with little downside to her.

Event: Stops working with Major Crimes after they return to West Baltimore
Explanation: Losing case involvement slightly limits her role but lets the specialized unit refocus on drug cases.

Event: McNulty begins to pursue Beadie at end of Season 3
Explanation: Pursuing a relationship offers emotional benefits to him and some potential benefit to her, with no implied harm yet.

Event: Beadie gets together with McNulty after he stops drinking
Explanation: Forming a relationship when he is stable benefits both emotionally and provides mutual support.

Event: Beadie’s relationship with McNulty becomes strained when he falls off the wagon
Explanation: Remaining in a now-dysfunctional relationship harms her well-being and offers little benefit to him.

Event: Beadie and McNulty (heavily implied) patch things up in the finale
Explanation: Reconciliation appears to restore mutual emotional support and stability for both.

Event: Initially treats port cop job as easy, low-effort gig
Explanation: She enjoys decent pay for minimal work, but the public gets less diligent policing.

Event: Later proves good at police work when major crime starts at the port
Explanation: Taking police work seriously improves her career and better protects the community.

Event: Does not take her job very seriously when first introduced
Explanation: She conserves her own effort and comfort at the cost of reduced service to the public.

Event: Develops into “real police” by end of the season
Explanation: Improved professionalism and competence advance her career and substantially aid law enforcement outcomes.
